_schema.purpose: Country -> region mapping for source-distribution visualization (Evidence Terrain) and source-balance analytics. Country values match the canonical names produced by src/stages/_helpers.py:normalise_country().
_schema.structure: Each bucket has: label (display name), countries (sorted list of canonical country names), and rationale (provenance + any divergence notes). Country lookup is forward (country -> bucket); no aliases here, use normalise_country() upstream.

_source: World Bank Regional Classification, 2023 vintage (https://datahelpdesk.worldbank.org/knowledgebase/articles/906519-world-bank-country-and-lending-groups). 7-region scheme.

turkey.rationale: Turkey's news ecosystem (Turkish-language outlets, regional alliances, conflict reporting horizon spanning Syria/Iraq/Cyprus/Greece) is more journalistically coherent with MENA than with European newsrooms. WB places Turkey in Europe & Central Asia for EU-candidacy and lending-operations reasons that do not reflect editorial geography.

afghanistan_pakistan.wb_assigns: Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan and Pakistan
afghanistan_pakistan.rationale: Afghanistan and Pakistan are linguistically (Urdu, Pashto, Dari), culturally, and historically South Asian. WB reassigned them from South Asia to a renamed MENA bucket in 2024 for lending-operations consolidation. That reassignment is unanchored in journalistic geography, where Afghanistan/Pakistan reporting is contiguous with India/Bangladesh/Sri Lanka coverage.

east_asia_pacific.label: East Asia & Pacific
east_asia_pacific.rationale: World Bank Regional Classification (2023). Includes ASEAN, Greater China, Japan, the Koreas, Australia, NZ, and Pacific island states.

europe_central_asia.label: Europe & Central Asia
europe_central_asia.rationale: World Bank Regional Classification (2023). Includes EU, EFTA, Western Balkans, EU candidates excluding Turkey, all post-Soviet states excluding Afghanistan.

latin_america_caribbean.label: Latin America & Caribbean
latin_america_caribbean.rationale: World Bank Regional Classification (2023). Includes Mexico, Central America, South America, and all Caribbean nations.

middle_east_north_africa.label: Middle East & North Africa
middle_east_north_africa.wb_region: Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan and Pakistan
middle_east_north_africa.rationale: World Bank Regional Classification (2023), with two documented divergences: (1) Turkey is included in MENA rather than Europe & Central Asia, reflecting cultural, linguistic, and journalistic affiliation with the Middle East — see _divergences.turkey. (2) Afghanistan and Pakistan are excluded from this bucket and assigned to South Asia, reflecting their pre-2024 WB classification and their cultural and linguistic affiliation with the South Asian subcontinent — see _divergences.afghanistan_pakistan.

north_america.label: North America
north_america.rationale: World Bank Regional Classification (2023). United States and Canada only. Mexico is in Latin America & Caribbean per WB.

south_asia.label: South Asia
south_asia.rationale: World Bank Regional Classification (2023), with one documented divergence: Afghanistan and Pakistan are included in South Asia, reflecting their pre-2024 WB classification and their cultural and linguistic affiliation with the subcontinent. WB reassigned them to MENA-AP in 2024 for lending-operations reasons; that reassignment does not reflect journalistic geography. See _divergences.afghanistan_pakistan.

sub_saharan_africa.label: Sub-Saharan Africa
sub_saharan_africa.rationale: World Bank Regional Classification (2023). All African countries south of the Sahara.
